model_narrative: We are planning train service on a railway network. For each candidate line we choose an integer service frequency, the number of trips that line runs per period, and for each origin-destination pair with demand we determine how many passengers can travel directly between those stations. The goal is to make the total number of direct travelers across all origin-destination pairs as large as possible.

sets.name: station
sets.doc: the railway stations in the network

sets.name: line
sets.doc: the candidate train lines, each named by its pair of endpoint stations; a line runs a fixed path through the network and a frequency is chosen for it

sets.name: edge
sets.doc: the network edges that carry a line-frequency requirement, each given as an ordered pair of stations

sets.name: odpair
sets.doc: the origin-destination pairs that have passenger demand, each given as an ordered pair of stations

sets.name: line_edge
sets.doc: membership records, each a quadruple whose first two entries name a line and whose last two entries name an edge that the line traverses; a line traverses an edge exactly when the matching quadruple is present

sets.name: rp_idx
sets.doc: membership records, each a triple whose first two entries name a line and whose third entry names a station that lies on that line; a station is served directly by a line exactly when the matching triple is present

params.name: maxtcap
params.doc: the maximum train capacity in passengers, a single scalar shared across the whole network

params.name: freq
params.doc: the required total line frequency on each edge, that is how many line-trips per period must cover that edge

params.name: od
params.doc: the passenger demand for each origin-destination pair, in passengers

params.name: rp
params.doc: the position rank of a station within a line; it records the ordering of stations along a line and is not used to size any quantity

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the integer frequency assigned to each candidate line, that is how many trips that line runs per period

vars.name: dt
vars.doc: the number of direct travelers served on each origin-destination pair; it is bounded above by that pair's passenger demand

description: On every edge that carries a frequency requirement, the trips supplied by the lines must exactly meet that requirement. For each such edge, add up the chosen frequencies of all the lines that travel over that edge and require this total to equal the edge's required frequency.
expected_pyomo:
def deffreqlop_rule(model, e1, e2):
    lines = [(la, lb) for (la, lb) in model.line if (la, lb, e1, e2) in model.line_edge]
    return sum(model.phi[la, lb] for (la, lb) in lines) == model.freq[e1, e2]
model.deffreqlop = Constraint(model.edge, rule=deffreqlop_rule)

The goal is to make the total number of direct travelers across all origin-destination pairs as large as possible.

params.name: maxtcap

vars.name: phi

vars.name: dt
vars.doc: the number of direct travelers served on each origin-destination pair; it is bounded above by that pair's passenger demand

description: The number of direct travelers carried between two stations is capped by the seats the serving lines provide. For each origin-destination pair, consider the lines that run directly through both the origin and the destination, and limit the direct travelers on that pair to the combined frequency of those lines times a per-trip seat figure, where that seat figure is the smaller of the pair's own passenger demand and the train capacity. When no line directly connects both stations, no direct travelers can be carried on that pair.

The goal is to make the total number of direct travelers across all origin-destination pairs as large as possible.

sets.doc: the railway stations in the network

sets.name: line
sets.doc: the candidate train lines, each named by its pair of endpoint stations; a line runs a fixed path through the network and a frequency is chosen for it

sets.name: edge

vars.name: dt
vars.doc: the number of direct travelers served on each origin-destination pair; it is bounded above by that pair's passenger demand

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, on every edge that carries a frequency requirement, make the combined frequency of the lines traveling over that edge exactly meet the edge's required frequency. Finally, for every origin-destination pair, cap the direct travelers carried on that pair by the combined frequency of the lines that pass directly through both stations, scaled by the smaller of that pair's passenger demand and the train capacity, allowing no direct travelers when no line connects both stations directly.
